name: Bottoms Up
description: A two-story timber-frame tavern on the corner of Mill Road and the King's Road. The sign out front is a painted wooden tankard tipped upside-down, faded by weather but recently re-hung. Inside it smells of woodsmoke, spilled ale, and whatever's simmering in the kitchen. The floorboards creak in specific places the regulars have memorized. The bar is oak, scarred and polished by decades of elbows. There are ten tables of varying size, a fireplace that dominates the west wall, and a staircase to the rooms upstairs that groans on the fourth step.

sounds: Creak of floorboards under serving staff
Pop and hiss of the hearth fire
Banter from the end of the bar where Norvin holds court
smells: Spilled ale soaked into old wood
Roasting meat or stew from the kitchen
Bread — Oma sends a basket over every morning
atmosphere.lighting: Candles on tables, oil lamps along the walls, firelight from the hearth. Dim in the corners. Warm amber everywhere else.

layout.name: Main Room
layout.description: The heart of the tavern. Ten tables, the bar along the north wall, fireplace to the west. Most of the town's informal business happens here.

layout.name: The Bar
layout.description: Oak counter with six stools. Samren's domain — the leonin presides from behind the bar with the pride of a former champion and the watchfulness of a recovering drinker. The end stool belongs to Norvin. Everyone knows this.

layout.name: Back Corner
layout.description: Two tables tucked behind a support pillar. Preferred by people who want to talk without being overheard. They are always overheard.

layout.name: Kitchen
layout.description: Small but efficient. A halfling-height prep table, an iron stove, hanging herbs, and a door to the alley for deliveries and smoke breaks.

layout.name: Upstairs Rooms
layout.description: Six rooms for rent. Clean, small, each with a bed, a basin, and a lock that actually works. The walls are thin.

layout.name: Third Floor
layout.description: Officially storage. Unofficially, a card table operates here two nights a week. The captain does not know about this.

layout.name: Cellar
layout.description: Kegs, bottles, root vegetables, and one locked cabinet from the previous management that Samren has not opened.

hooks: The card game on the third floor — anyone with the right introduction can get in
Fen Colby has been drinking more than usual and muttering about something he saw at the docks
A traveler left a locked chest in one of the rooms two weeks ago and hasn't come back
A locked cabinet from the previous owner — Samren has not opened it and does not plan to
The bread basket from Oma's bakery arrives before dawn and is always empty by mid-morning

worldContext.location: Bottoms Up tavern, Mill Road and King's Road, Millhaven
